prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Imagine your brain being a machine that can perform lots and lots of calculations in a blink of an eye. Now, imagine we have a machine that is designed specifically to perform lots and lots of calculations, but it can also become confused and perform incorrectly if it's doing all these calculations simultaneously. That's what this new organ we are calling a CPU does - it performs lots of calculations and can do several calculations simultaneously without getting confused. CPU stands for "Central Processing Unit" - it's the core part of not only your computer but also your smartphone and a lot of other devices that perform lots of calculations − that's what this new organ we are calling a CPU does. It’s able to perform lots of calculations and also do several calculations simultaneously without getting confused. That's why CPU stands for "Central Processing Unit" − it's the core part of not only your computer but also your smartphone and a lot of other devices that perform lots of calculations.
